title: rubus villicaulis subsp. castellarnaui
intro: Rubus villicaulis subsp. castellarnaui, also known as the Madeira raspberry or Madeira dewberry, is a species of flowering plant in the rose family that is native to the island of Madeira in Portugal. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, making it useful for treating various health issues. The health benefits of Rubus villicaulis subsp. castellarnaui include its potential to lower blood sugar levels, improve cardiovascular health, and exhibit antimicrobial properties. Herbal preparations of this plant are often made into teas, infusions, or tinctures to take advantage of its medicinal properties, although more research is needed to fully understand its effects and potential uses.
